refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable achieving the customer-defined productivity target by optimizing equipment operation, task scheduling, and resource allocation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is broad and high-level; decomposing it into sub-FRs focused on key productivity drivers (equipment operation efficiency, task scheduling, resource allocation) allows targeted design and verification.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure productivity metrics (m³/h or ton/h) during representative operations; verify that each sub-FR contributes to meeting or exceeding the productivity target.
sub_frs: FR1: Optimize equipment operation efficiency to maximize throughput.
FR2: Schedule tasks effectively to minimize idle time and delays.
FR3: Allocate resources dynamically to support continuous productivity.

candidate_dps: DP1: Advanced vehicle control algorithms to improve operation efficiency.
DP2: Intelligent task scheduling system integrated with teleoperation.
DP3: Dynamic resource management services within the NGHE solution.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Advanced vehicle control algorithms to improve operation efficiency.

potential_sources: Interaction between vehicle control and task scheduling modules.
coupling_check.notes: DP1 is logically decoupled from task scheduling and resource management but requires telemetry input; interfaces are well-defined.

assumptions_risks: Assumes telemetry data is accurate and timely.
Risk of performance degradation if vehicle control algorithms are not optimized.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating task scheduling DP in next decomposition level to address coupling more explicitly.

im_boundary.name: Vehicle
im_boundary.scope: Physical and software components responsible for vehicle operation and control.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives real-time vehicle status and environment data.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives scheduled tasks and commands from teleoperation or NGHE solution.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Bidirectional communication channel for control commands and status updates.

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment of vehicle control DP; low diffusion as control logic is centralized.
Limited containment; mostly interfaces with vehicle control.
Minimal containment; safety logic separate but interacts via alerts.
Moderate containment; overarching system coordination but not direct vehicle control.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Vehicle module is the best architectural boundary to contain the advanced vehicle control DP due to physical proximity and centralized control logic.
containment_risks: Potential interface complexity with Teleoperation and NGHE Solution modules.
Risk of diffusion if control algorithms are duplicated outside Vehicle module.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Ensure strict interface definitions and code ownership policies to prevent diffusion.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP logically satisfies the refined FR by focusing on vehicle operation efficiency, a key productivity driver.

diffusion_risks: Possible duplication of control algorithms across Vehicle and Teleoperation modules.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Vehicle module shows strong containment scores and low diffusion, supporting good architectural packaging.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed by measuring productivity metrics and monitoring telemetry data from the Vehicle module.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of DP to Vehicle IM with well-defined interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Plan next decomposition level to address task scheduling and resource allocation FRs and their DP/IM mappings.
Define interface contracts explicitly to mitigate diffusion risks.

decision.justification: While the decomposition and assignments are sound, the current level only addresses one sub-FR (equipment operation efficiency). To fully meet the productivity KPI, the other sub-FRs (task scheduling and resource allocation) must be decomposed and assigned.
next_level_sub_frs: FR2: Schedule tasks effectively to minimize idle time and delays.
FR3: Allocate resources dynamically to support continuous productivity.

revise.why: To include full decomposition of productivity KPI into all relevant sub-FRs for comprehensive design coverage.

assumptions: Telemetry data is reliable and timely.
Vehicle control algorithms can be centralized within Vehicle module.
open_risks: Interface complexity and potential diffusion with Teleoperation and NGHE Solution modules.
Incomplete coverage of productivity drivers at this level.
evidence_needed: Performance data from vehicle control algorithms during representative operations.
Interface compliance and ownership enforcement documentation.
